detail: Stem is a difference-of-squares factoring/cancellation, not an exponent/radical-rule application. Better fits A2.T5 or A3.T8. Answer x^5-5 is correct.

Single radical-to-power conversion with perfect exponents (cbrt(p^3 q^6)=pq^2) is an easy-lever item; medium requires multi-step rule chain or rational-exponent <-> radical with non-trivial indices. Answer pq^2 is correct.
Deliverable is a numeric value of an unknown constant c via coefficient matching; per template skill_wide_notes this belongs to A4.T9, not A1.T10 substitute-and-simplify. Answer c=1 is correct.

Item asks to identify b^(7/15) rewritten by multiplying numerator and denominator of the exponent by 15; no actual exponent/radical rule is exercised (just multiplying the fraction by 1). Stem is trivial and doesn't match T7 medium characteristics. Answer (b) is mathematically equivalent but the cognitive demand is mismatched.

Medium T2 lever requires subtraction (distribute negative across second polynomial); this clone is pure addition of two polynomials with small integer coefficients, matching the easy lever. Answer 11w^5+7w^2 is correct.

Deliverable is a numeric value of unknown constant c via matching to cy^4-11; per template skill_wide_notes this belongs to A4.T9 (16de54c7 example in T9), not A1.T2 simple add/subtract. Answer c=10 is correct.
